title: Newrest Nigeria Plc Surprises Shareholders with N6.85 Kobo Final Dividend - FinancialEDGE
description: Newrest Nigeria Plc has surprised its shareholders with a final dividend of N6.85 kobo for the financial year ended December 31, 2023. This is in addition to the interim dividend of N2.18 kobo per share paid in March 2023 and N3.86 kobo per share paid in November 2023. Newrest Nigeria Plc's shares have become highly
html:
Newrest Nigeria Plc has surprised its shareholders with a final dividend of N6.85 kobo for the financial year ended December 31, 2023.

This is in addition to the interim dividend of N2.18 kobo per share paid in March 2023 and N3.86 kobo per share paid in November 2023.

Newrest Nigeria Plc’s shares have become highly sought after, with prospective shareholders eager to buy into the company’s success. However, existing shareholders are holding tight, unwilling to sell their shares, making the stock extremely scarce on the NASD OTC exchange.

Despite the challenges faced by the aviation industry, including the removal of fuel subsidy, high cost of aviation fuel, and devaluation of the naira, Newrest Nigeria Plc recorded a turnover of N9.9 billion, up from N6.2 billion in 2022. The company’s consolidated profit stood at N7.5 billion, compared to N2.2 billion in 2022.

The company’s chairman, Richard Akerele, attributed the success to innovative cost management strategies and a focus on quality.

The managing director, Alexis Probst, said the company would continue to prioritize strategic goals and prudent expenditure management to maintain its position in the inflight catering business.

The company has a rich history, dating back to its incorporation as a private limited liability company on December 6, 1995.

It later became a public limited liability company on February 26, 2007, and was listed on the Nigerian Stock Exchange (NSE) on July 25, 2007.

In 2019, the company underwent a delisting process, which was formalized by the NSE on May 13, 2019.

Despite the challenges in the aviation industry, Newrest Nigeria Plc’s strong financial performance and commitment to quality have made it an attractive investment opportunity, leading to the surge in demand for its shares

Newrest Nigeria Plc operates in-flight catering facilities and VIP lounges at the Murital Muhammed International Airport Lagos and the Nnamdi Azikiwe International Airport, Abuja, and has three subsidiaries providing catering and logistics services to the oil & gas sector, industries, schools, and domestic charter flights.
